esp32c3_supermini.label: ESP32-C3 SuperMini
esp32c3_supermini.notes: Compact form factor. GPIO8/9 are default I2C. GPIO20/21 are UART0 (USB). Strapping pins: GPIO2, GPIO8, GPIO9.

GPIO2.notes: Strapping pin — avoid pulling low at boot

GPIO9.notes: Default I2C SCL. Strapping pin — internal pull-up at boot.

GPIO20.notes: UART0 RX — used for USB serial

GPIO21.notes: UART0 TX — used for USB serial

esp32_devkit.label: ESP32 DevKit V1
esp32_devkit.notes: Classic ESP32. 38-pin variant. GPIO6-11 connected to internal flash — do not use.

GPIO0.notes: Strapping pin — must be HIGH at boot for normal mode

GPIO2.notes: Strapping pin — must be LOW or floating at boot

GPIO12.notes: Strapping pin — must be LOW at boot

esp32s2_devkit.label: ESP32-S2 DevKit
esp32s2_devkit.notes: Single-core. Native USB. No Bluetooth. Good for USB HID projects.

esp32s3_devkit.label: ESP32-S3 DevKit
esp32s3_devkit.notes: Dual-core with AI acceleration. Native USB OTG. Best ESP32 for ML edge tasks.

esp32c6_devkit.label: ESP32-C6 DevKit
esp32c6_devkit.notes: WiFi 6, Zigbee, Thread, Matter support. Two cores: HP (160MHz) and LP (low power).

esp32e22_devkit.label: ESP32-E22 DevKit
esp32e22_devkit.notes: 2026 Flagship. Tri-band Wi-Fi 6E (2.4/5/6GHz). High-speed throughput up to 2.1 Gbps.

esp32p4_devkit.label: ESP32-P4 Function-EV-Board
esp32p4_devkit.notes: High-performance multimedia SoC. No integrated wireless (Wi-Fi/BT). Features MIPI-DSI, MIPI-CSI, H.264, and Ethernet.

esp32c5_devkit.label: ESP32-C5 DevKitC-1
esp32c5_devkit.notes: First dual-band (2.4/5GHz) Wi-Fi 6 ESP32. v1.2 revision.

esp32h2_devkit.label: ESP32-H2 DevKitM-1
esp32h2_devkit.notes: Low-power RISC-V with Bluetooth 5.4, Zigbee, Thread, and Matter support. v1.2 revision.

esp32c6_supermini.label: ESP32-C6 SuperMini
esp32c6_supermini.notes: Compact Wi-Fi 6, BLE 5, and Zigbee/Thread board. Pinout follows QSZNTEC layout.

esp32c2_devkit.label: ESP32-C2 DevKitM-1 (ESP8684)
esp32c2_devkit.notes: Entry-level Wi-Fi + BLE chip (ESP8684). v1.1 revision.

arduino_uno.label: Arduino Uno R3
arduino_uno.notes: The classic Arduino board. 5V logic. Built-in LED on D13.

arduino_nano.label: Arduino Nano
arduino_nano.notes: Small, breadboard-friendly version of the Uno. 5V logic.

arduino_micro.label: Arduino Micro
arduino_micro.notes: Compact board with native USB (ATmega32U4). Can act as HID device (keyboard/mouse). 5V logic. Built-in LED on D13.

arduino_leonardo.label: Arduino Leonardo
arduino_leonardo.notes: Full-size board with native USB via ATmega32U4. Can emulate HID devices. 5V logic. Hardware Serial1 on D0/D1; USB handles Serial.

arduino_mega_2560.label: Arduino Mega 2560
arduino_mega_2560.notes: Large-format board with 54 digital I/O pins, 16 analog inputs, and 4 hardware UARTs. 5V logic. Built-in LED on D13.

arduino_nano_every.label: Arduino Nano Every
arduino_nano_every.notes: Updated Nano form factor with ATmega4809. 5V logic, more flash/RAM than classic Nano. Pinout is Nano-compatible but some peripheral mappings differ.

A6.notes: Analog input; digital I/O supported unlike classic Nano

A7.notes: Analog input; digital I/O supported unlike classic Nano

arduino_zero.label: Arduino Zero
arduino_zero.notes: 32-bit ARM Cortex-M0+ board. 3.3V logic — NOT 5V tolerant. Two USB ports: Native USB and Programming/Debug (EDBG). Built-in LED on D13.

arduino_mkr_wifi_1010.label: Arduino MKR WiFi 1010
arduino_mkr_wifi_1010.notes: Compact MKR-form-factor board with built-in WiFi and Bluetooth via u-blox NINA-W102. 3.3V logic — NOT 5V tolerant. Onboard LiPo charger. Built-in LED on D6.

D13.notes: SPI CS; also used for SD on some shields

5V.notes: 5V output from USB or VIN regulator
